meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve overwhelmed by AI tools (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome overwhelmed by AI tools. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: overwhelmed by AI tools

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Master AI Development for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop drowning in AI tools. Learn to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship high-value projects faster. Perfect for freelance devs.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing every new AI tool. The secret to professional velocity is a standardized SaaS starter kit. By pairing Antigravity’s structural scaffolding with Claude Code’s autonomous execution, you transform from an overwhelmed tinkerer into a high-throughput developer capable of shipping production-ready SaaS applications in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
The modern freelance landscape has turned into a chaotic arena of endless AI tool subscriptions, fragmented workflows, and feature fatigue. You spend more time configuring environments and context-switching between LLMs than actually shipping value to clients. The weight of 'keeping up' is paradoxically slowing you down, leaving your project pipeline stagnant while your competitors automate their way to profitability.

But the chaos is optional. The elite 1% of AI builders don't rely on a dozen disparate tools; they rely on a battle-tested SaaS starter kit—an architectural blueprint that handles the heavy lifting of authentication, database schema, and UI primitives. By integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for autonomous logic implementation, you can reclaim your time and deliver premium results that command higher fees.

steps.title: Define the Architecture
steps.description: Establish the foundation by defining your tech stack. We need a clean, modular structure that supports rapid iteration without the bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a modern SaaS starter kit using Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, Supabase for auth/DB, and Stripe integration. Ensure a modular directory structure that separates business logic from UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --init 'Set up project structure based on Next.js 14 and Supabase architecture'

steps.title: Configure Authentication and User Roles
steps.description: Don't reinvent the wheel. Use your starter kit to bake in secure, multi-tenant authentication immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement Supabase Auth with RLS policies that enforce organization-level data isolation. Include a middleware for protected routes and user role management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure Supabase Auth middleware and set up Row Level Security policies for user tenants'

steps.title: Automate Database Schema Migration
steps.description: Speed up the development process by letting AI generate your schema based on your project requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SQL schema for a SaaS platform including tables for profiles, subscriptions, and usage_logs. Ensure proper foreign key relationships.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply SQL schema migrations to Supabase and generate TypeScript types from the database'

steps.title: Build High-Conversion UI Components
steps.description: Use your starter kit's design system to create consistent, professional UI components without custom CSS headaches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive dashboard landing page using Shadcn UI components. Include a pricing table and feature comparison grid.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Build the dashboard layout using existing Shadcn components and Tailwind utility classes'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Finalize your build pipeline so you can ship to production in one click.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file and setup GitHub Actions for automated linting and testing on every push.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure Vercel deployment pipeline and add GitHub Actions for CI/CD'

pro_tips: Treat your starter kit as a product, not a project; update it every time you build a successful feature to compound your efficiency.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to store your project style guide so you never have to repeat UI preferences.
Prioritize 'feature flags' in your starter kit; it allows you to ship code to clients before the feature is fully complete.
Always run your schema changes through a local dev container before applying to production to avoid catastrophic migration errors.
Automate your client documentation; use AI to generate READMEs and API specs directly from your codebase after every major sprint.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, create a 'Client-Ready' branch. Every time you onboard a new client, clone this branch to skip the setup phase entirely and move straight to feature development.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours on boilerplate and start delivering value that clients actually pay for. By standardizing your workflow, you create a scalable, repeatable, and highly profitable freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
